hypothesis: If a discrete stochastic autoencoder is trained end-to-end to map backbone coordinates to discrete sequences and back to all-atom structures (E2EFOLD), then it will produce designed sequences whose predicted folded structures closely match the input backbones (higher TM-score and lower RMSD) and achieve superior sequence recovery and self-consistency compared to decoupled pipelines or models ablated of structural loss terms, because joint end-to-end optimization enforces tighter sequence–structure compatibility.
